verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes the inducible Hsp70 chaperone, primarily cytosolic. However, surface translocation is well-documented under stress conditions (heat shock, oxidative stress, tumor transformation): Hsp70 appears on the outer plasma membrane leaflet of stressed and malignant cells, where it acts as an NK-cell activating ligand and tumor immunotherapy target. This stress-induced surface exposure — without a classical TM anchor, but via direct membrane association — represents a documented cell-state-induced surface pool. Multiple CAR-T and antibody programs exploit this surface Hsp70 display.
